items.title: Thursday's Digital Daily: July 16, 2026
items.summary: WWD's daily trade digest covers the second chapter of Gucci's Monte Carlo summer campaign, spotlighting the Jackie bag reworked in metallic mesh and a shimmering Dionysus as the lead accessories, continuing the Demna-directed campaign shot by Mark Seliger across Monaco.

items.title: Oliver Chen Maintains Hold on Kering Amid Gucci Headwinds and Macro Volatility, Trimming Price Target to EUR290
items.summary: TD Cowen analyst Oliver Chen trimmed Kering's price target to EUR290 from EUR300 while keeping a Hold rating, citing continued Gucci headwinds. He expects some sequential operational improvement but still forecasts Gucci sales to stay in negative territory, flagging softer China trends and persistent Middle East pressure as key risks.

items.title: L'Oreal Lands Gucci Beauty Early as Coty Cashes Out for US$400M
items.summary: L'Oreal secured a 50-year exclusive global license for Gucci Beauty effective July 1, 2027, a year ahead of the original 2028 expiration. Coty receives about $400 million (split between cash at signing and a deferred 2027 payment) to exit early; L'Oreal frames the deal as building 'a new multi-billion-euro house' combining Gucci's creative vision with its distribution scale.

items.title: Forum Members React to the 2026 Gucci Monte Carlo Summer Campaign
items.summary: Community discussion thread aggregating fashion-forum reactions to the Monte Carlo campaign is genuinely split. One member praised "the sense of polish, luxury, and unapologetic glamour," singling out Amelia Gray and Emma Mae Koch's shots, while others called the execution "very cheap and dated" and a "patchwork" of mismatched, tacky production choices.

items.title: Kering SA (KER:FP) (PPRUY) PT Lowered to EUR290 at TD Cowen
items.summary: Wire item confirming TD Cowen's price-target trim to EUR290 on Kering shares, part of a Hold-rating note flagging ongoing Gucci sales softness and macro volatility across key regions.

items.title: Luca de Meo Touts Potential of Jewelry, Gucci at Kering Annual Meeting
items.summary: At Kering's shareholder meeting, CEO Luca de Meo reiterated confidence in the Gucci turnaround under Demna, highlighted plans to grow the jewelry and watches category from EUR200 million toward roughly EUR700 million, and repeated the ambition to more than double group profitability by 2030.

items.title: PurseForum Gucci Subforum - Ongoing Member Threads
items.summary: Active PurseForum Gucci subforum continues running threads on Demna-era pricing, bag material quality (canvas versus leather durability questions), and mixed reception of reissued archive styles; regular buyers note wanting to see more creative effort before committing to new purchases at current price points.

items.title: Kering's $3.9 Billion Quarter Shows Gucci Isn't Fixed Yet
items.summary: Analysis piece noting Gucci's comparable sales fell 8% in Q1 2026, an 11th consecutive quarterly decline, dragging down Kering's fashion and leather goods division even as North American demand helped other house brands (Saint Laurent, Bottega Veneta, Balenciaga) grow.

items.title: Gucci Horsebit Loafer Styling Content Trending on TikTok
items.summary: Continued creator volume around Gucci Horsebit loafer styling and unboxing content under NA-facing hashtags (#gucci #gucciloafer #luxuryshoes); creators pair the loafers with both classic and streetwear fits, sustaining steady organic SNS visibility for a heritage silhouette. Exact per-video engagement counts were not accessible via the discovery page.

items.title: Gucci's Prospects Are Brightening, Including for Fragrance: Analysts
items.summary: Equity analysts turned incrementally more constructive on Gucci (39% of Kering group sales) ahead of Demna's product cadence ramping up; TD Cowen projected 2% H1 growth and 6% H2 growth, while Barclays flagged upside for a future Gucci fragrance franchise once L'Oreal assumes the beauty license in 2027.
